exemplar.title: How does Marjane Satrapiâs usage of visual and literary techniques in âPersepolisâ explore female gender norms in identity thereby critiquing the impacts of war on self-discovery?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Marjane Satrapiâs usage of visual and literary techniques in âPersepolisâ explore female gender norms in identity thereby critiquing the impacts of war on self-discovery?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Sustained connection of key plot events to the guiding concept of identity
Relevant visual and verbal references to support ideas
Regular linking of contextual details (e.g., veil, school expulsion) to gender norms
weaknesses: Interpretation remains fairly general rather than deeply persuasive
Tendency to summarise rather than explore deeper ideological or historical implications
Limited engagement with secondary sources or scholarly criticism
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates satisfactory knowledge and understanding of Persepolis, consistently linking narrative events to identity and using mostly relevant references. However, the treatment of implications remains at a general level and lacks the depth and persuasion required for higher bands. On balance, a score of 3 reflects sustained interpretation with room to develop more incisive analysis and broader contextual grounding.

strengths: Insightful reading of gaze and power dynamics in specific panels
Identification of symbolism and graphic contrast to comment on alienation and patriarchy
Occasional evaluative points linking authorial choices to reader response
weaknesses: Frequent lapses into description and generic statements
Surface-level claims without fully unpacking techniqueâeffect relationships
Lack of precision in referring to panels and text elements hampers depth of evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student offers generally appropriate analysis and some evaluative insightâparticularly in reading gaze, symbolism, and contrastâbut several passages rely on description or oversimplified claims. To reach a higher band, the evaluation of visual techniques needed to be consistently precise and probing. A band-3 score fairly captures this mix of strengths and descriptive weaknesses.

strengths: Clear overall structure with introduction, themed sections, and conclusion
Explicit statement of focus concept maintains cohesion
Topic sentences signpost movement through key arguments
weaknesses: Uneven development and abrupt shifts between ideas
Supporting examples are integrated mechanically rather than woven seamlessly
Thesis and concluding synthesis lack conciseness and broader implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The response is adequately organized, maintaining focus on the research question and showing a coherent line of inquiry, but development is uneven and transitions can feel abrupt. Integration of evidence is sometimes mechanical, and the essay would benefit from a sharper thesis and synthesized conclusion. These factors align with a band-3 performance.

strengths: Use of varied academic vocabulary in places
Generally formal register appropriate for literary analysis
weaknesses: Persistent syntactic awkwardness and complex sentence structures impede clarity
Frequent grammar and diction errors distract the reader
Occasional informal phrasing disrupts academic tone
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the language shows moments of clarity and appropriate academic register, recurring grammatical lapses, awkward constructions, and diction errors hinder readability. These frequent issues place the writing at a band-2 level for language clarity and accuracy, balancing occasional strengths against pervasive lapses.

strandSelections.reasoning: Examples (panels, short quotations) are regularly embedded, but integration is mechanical at times, with images inserted mid-paragraph and commentary added afterwards instead of woven smoothly into sentences. Citations are minimal (only one entry in Works Cited). Thus, supporting material is âsometimesâ rather than âmostly wellâ integrated.

The student recounts key plot points from both the Iranian and Austrian sections of Persepolis and continually links them to the guiding concept of identity. References to the veil, school expulsion, the sexual-revolution party and the minefield scene show a satisfactory grasp of context and purpose, and the commentary regularly connects these events to gender norms and self-discovery. However, the discussion remains fairly generalâoften summarising what happens rather than exploring deeper ideological or historical implicationsâso the interpretation is sustained but not yet âpersuasiveâ or consistently insightful.

The piece follows a clear structure (introduction, thematic sections, conclusion) and maintains focus on the research question. Topic sentences signpost movement from cultural norms to gender expectations to warâs impact. Development, however, can be uneven; some paragraphs combine multiple ideas or shift abruptly, and the line of inquiry is asserted more than systematically built. Cohesion is adequate rather than sophisticated.

Panels are reproduced in the essay and the narration points to specific visual details (colour contrast, facial expressions, body language). Short verbal quotations such as âWe found ourselves veiled and separatedâ and âwhatâs the difference between you and a whoreâ are used to back up claims. These references are generally relevant and usually placed close to the analytical point, but they are brief, sometimes paraphrased, and not always unpacked in depth. Hence they âmostlyâ rather than âeffectivelyâ support the argument.

The essay generally maintains an objective, formal register appropriate for literary analysis, using third-person references and academic terminology (e.g., âjuxtaposition,â âsymbolismâ). Occasional informal phrasing (âMarii (her childhood name),â âone could argueâ) and contracted forms prevent the register from being consistently polished, placing it best within the âmostly appropriateâ band.

Vocabulary is varied and academic; most sentences are clear. Nevertheless, syntactic awkwardness (âFurthermore through the use of graphic contrastâ¦ The Iran-Iraq conflict in Persepolis critiquesâ) and agreement errors (âgender involvement in identity commonly fairs the female worseâ) occur throughout. These lapses impede flow but do not obscure meaning, matching the âclear with adequate accuracy despite some lapsesâ descriptor.

The essay goes beyond description by identifying graphic contrast, symbolism, gaze and weighting, then commenting on how these choices construct alienation or reinforce patriarchy. Still, several passages lapse into simple retelling (e.g., âThis acts to increase the memorability of this panelâ) or generic statements (âblack stands for power, fear, and evilâ) without probing techniqueâeffect relationships in depth. Overall the analysis is appropriate and occasionally evaluative, but not consistently insightful.

annotations.comment: Explicitly stating "Focus concept: Identity" demonstrates clear alignment and focus throughout the essay. This signals to the reader the central lens for all subsequent analysis and supports a cohesive argument.

The research question is clearly stated and focused, outlining both visual and literary techniques and their relation to gender norms and warâs impact on identity. This provides a solid foundation for analysis but could be enhanced by defining key terms like "visual techniques" and "literary techniques" more precisely.
The interpretation of gaze reinforcing societal expectations is compelling and shows awareness of narrative framing. This is a well-considered analysis of author's visual choices.
The proximity of image and text feels disjointed. Integrate discussion of this figure more smoothly by embedding analytical commentary within the same paragraph as the image reference.
This opening paragraph is ambitious but overly long and syntactically complex. Break into shorter sentences and clarify subject-verb relationships to improve clarity and reduce reader fatigue.
The thesis is implied rather than explicitly stated. Consider ending the introduction with a concise thesis sentence that directly answers the question and previews your main analytical points.
The transition to the school expulsion example is abrupt. Add a clear topic sentence explaining how this panel illustrates the broader theme of identity conflict under enforced norms.
When referencing âThe panel below,â specify the panel number or caption for precise citation. This will strengthen your use of visual evidence and improve reference clarity.
Including the panel image here effectively grounds your visual analysis. The sizing and placement are strong choices that direct reader attention to your discussion.

The claim that black-and-white iconography simply âlets readers step into Marjâs shoesâ is superficial. Explain how the stark palette specifically affects reader perception of defiance and identity.
Unpack the techniqueâeffect relationship more fully. For example, discuss how absence of text in panel four shifts narrative voice and intensifies emotional resonance.
The analysis on cultural juxtaposition is promising, but the sentence ends abruptly with âonly by lightly.â Proofread to complete the idea and clarify its significance.

Facial expressions and graphic contrast are noted, but identify a specific panel and describe which expression communicates alienation. This precision will strengthen your evaluation of authorial choice.
The discussion of graphic contrast is valid but remains at a descriptive level. Analyze how contrast influences reader interpretation of Marjaneâs emotional state to deepen the critique.

The phrasing âGender involvement in identity commonly fairs the female worseâ contains a diction error. Replace âfairsâ with âfaresâ and revise for clarity to maintain formal academic tone.
Analysis of the guardâs panel effectively highlights how visual choices comment on power dynamics. The connection between background color and regime symbolism shows insightful evaluation.

Stating that black âstands for power, fear, and evilâ is general. Consider referencing a secondary source on visual semiotics or explain how cultural associations in Iranian settings reinforce these connotations.
The quotation âI was shocked.â appears without sufficient narrative lead-in. Provide context for when and why Marjane felt this shock to strengthen the link between technique and effect.
The term âverbal conflictâ is vague. Specify the dialogue or text elements being analyzed and explain how they disrupt identity construction to add precision to your evaluation.
Your description of silhouettes and motion lines demonstrates strong observation of visual technique. This effectively connects form and content to underscore the tragedy of war.
The phrase âinapt personalityâ is unclear. Choose a more precise term or explain what qualities make the personality âinaptâ to clarify your argument.
This detailed narrative offers strong context but shifts into summary. Refocus by highlighting how Satrapiâs descriptive strategies convey identity transformations rather than retelling events.

The concluding section restates points but lacks a succinct synthesis and broader implications. Add a final sentence that ties back to the research question and emphasizes the relevance of your findings.
Introducing arranged marriages here feels abrupt, as it brings in new content at the end. Consider integrating this discussion earlier or removing it to maintain cohesion.

A single entry in Works Cited limits the scope of your research foundation. Include at least page references for citations and consider adding scholarly articles or criticism to support deeper analysis.
The "Works cited" heading is correctly formatted and clearly distinguishes the citation section.
